prompt: WRITE A SPEC ONLY (no build). Use the spec-writer skill (Skill tool: appydave:spec-writer). REQUIREMENT — 'Self-learning as active machinery: the learning-sweep guardrail'. Read these sources as input: memory files at ~/.claude/projects/-Users-davidcruwys-dev-ad-apps-dark-factory/memory/{self-learning-every-conversation.md, build-reusable-systems-not-one-offs.md, lead-with-my-take-not-question-menus.md, monitors-survive-compaction.md} + the canonical hooks spec at ~/dev/ad/brains/anthropic-claude/claude-code/hooks/INDEX.md. KEY PROBLEM the spec must solve: the TRIGGER is the weak link, not storage. Memories exist but DON'T FIRE — EVIDENCE: in one session (2026-06-08) recorded rules were violated 3-4x despite being written down (the 'lead with my-take not menus' rule; the 'don't manage AngelEye' rule). Per the Millwright lesson, the fix is ACTIVE MACHINERY, not another passive memory. Spec a mechanism (a skill and/or a hook — no cron, Max plan) that FORCES the learning sweep: detect a trigger (milestone / a correction from David / pre-compaction / session-end) -> perform-or-prompt the persist (to auto-memory / MEMORY.md / backlog) -> verify against the litmus 'if David restarted now, would the next session lose anything material?'. Consider PreCompact/Stop/SessionStart hooks (read the hooks spec for the right events), a marshall self-learning sub-routine, and how to avoid nagging. The spec must define: the triggers, the sweep procedure, where things persist, acceptance criteria (testable — e.g. a correction in a session results in a persisted memory before session-end), NFRs, and OPEN PO DECISIONS (e.g. hook-enforced vs skill-discipline). Build-technique-agnostic; building LATER. WRITE the spec to backlog/specs/self-learning-guardrail-spec.md. Then complete run-next-workflow bookkeeping (run record + done/ + reports/ handback — do not skip the handback).
